content: Autonomous agents using ICM curiosity mechanisms can bias their exploration to prioritize discoveries that compress new information into the 3–5 chunk working memory capacity limit that bounds LLM reasoning, creating a self-regulating curiosity loop that avoids information overload while still driving consistent learning. This bias aligns intrinsic reward generation with the agent's inherent reasoning bottleneck, rather than rewarding all novel experience indiscriminately.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
